climate_milestone: "Dry summer with moderate water stress. Rapid ripening in August."
"Severe spring frosts that reduced production. Cycle recovered by a warm August."
"Cool and cloudy summer. Very slow ripening; one of the latest harvests."
"Persistent rains during late ripening. Controlled botrytis risk."
"Climatologically perfect year. Ideal heat summation and cool nights in September."
"Prolonged drought. The vineyard advanced ripening to survive the heat."
"Long vegetative cycle. Mild summer that allowed record volume production."
"Extremely hot August. Stormy instability at the beginning of September."
"Significant water deficit. Phenolic ripening accelerated by lack of water."
"Humid end of cycle. Excess vigor in the plant that delayed alcoholic grade."
"Year of great water balance. Fluid harvest without climatic incidents."
"Dry summer with very cold nights in September. Mythic vintage for color concentration."
"Irregular cycle with cold spring. Summer with little insolation that delayed sugar."
"Historic heatwave in Europe. Extreme thermal stress; emergency harvest due to grade."
"Classic Mediterranean climate. Slow and progressive ripening; excellent health."
"Very dry cycle. Total absence of cryptogamic diseases (fungi) due to lack of rain."
"Torrid July followed by a milder August that balanced the plant."
"Atlantic year. Cool and with very slow ripening. Wines of great acidity."
"Rainy spring and cool summer. Generalized delay in all subzones."
"Heat stroke at the end of August that triggered ripening in Rioja Oriental."
"Exceptional light conditions in September. Perfect phenolic ripening."
"Warm and early year. Harvest generalized in Rioja Alta earlier than usual."
"Historic extreme drought. Very small berries with high tannin concentration."
"Coldest year of the century. Vegetative delay of 3 weeks; frost risk at harvest."
"Complicated September due to storms. Required highly technical selection in the winery."
"Heatwave in June/July. Cycle advancement due to heat summation accumulation."
"Dry and very long summer. Absence of rains in October allowed selective harvest."
"Devastating "black" frost in April + Warmest summer of the series. Maximum earliness."
"Year of frequent storms. Great mildew pressure that required constant vigilance."
"Scarce but high-quality fruit set. Accelerated ripening due to a radiant August."
"Cycle very advanced due to warm spring. Harvest marked by COVID protocols."
"Ideal thermal contrast between day and night. Very balanced and fresh ripening."
"Heatwave record. Advancement of budding and ripening due to extreme thermal stress."
"Extreme heat episodes in August. Gap between sugar and skin ripening."
"(Estimated) Humid winter and summer with intermittent thermal peaks."
"Year of Extreme Contrasts: Extremely rainy spring followed by an August with two consecutive heatwaves and unusually warm nights."
